output:
Looking at this causality problem:

**Given information:**
- P(visited England) = 49% = 0.49
- P(not visited England AND fired) = 14% = 0.14
- P(visited England AND fired) = 37% = 0.37

**Calculate conditional probabilities:**

P(fired | not visited England) = P(not visited AND fired) / P(not visited) = 0.14 / 0.51 ≈ **27.5%**

P(fired | visited England) = P(visited AND fired) / P(visited) = 0.37 / 0.49 ≈ **75.5%**

**No.** The chance of the employee being fired is **larger** when observing having visited England (75.5% vs 27.5%).
